About: It is about a Swedish pop group formed in Stockholm in 1972.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Agnetha Fältskog, Björn Ulvaeus, Benny Andersson, and Anni-Frid Lyngstad.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the history of popular music.

HasA: It has a digital avatar concert tour.
It has a new album released on 5 November 2021.
It has a concert residency featuring virtual avatars.
It has eight consecutive number-one albums in the UK.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being among the most renowned and commercially successful musical groups in history.
It is characterized by winning the Eurovision Song Contest for Sweden with their song "Waterloo" in 1974.
It is characterized by having their personal lives suffer as fame grew, leading to the dissolution of both marriages.
It is characterized by later music featuring darker and more introspective lyrics.
It is characterized by selling in excess of 400 million records worldwide.
It is characterized by being the first group from a non-English-speaking country to achieve consistent success on the charts in English-speaking countries.
It is characterized by being recognised as the best-selling Swedish band of all time.
It is characterized by being the best-selling band originating from continental Europe.
It is characterized by significant success in Latin America, recording a collection of their hit songs in Spanish.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by relationship changes reflected in later music.
It was influenced by the success of their music adapted into Mamma Mia!.
It was influenced by the success of the film Mamma Mia!.
IsA: It is a Swedish pop group.
KnownFor: It is known for being among the most renowned and commercially successful musical groups in history.
It is known for winning the Eurovision Song Contest in 1974.
It is known for selling in excess of 400 million records worldwide.
It is known for the compilation album "Abba Gold: Greatest Hits".
It is known for the stage musical Mamma Mia!.
It is known for the film Mamma Mia!.
It is known for the sequel film Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again.
It is known for the Voyage album.
It is known for the ABBA Voyage concert residency.
LocatedIn: It is located in the United Kingdom.
MemberOf: It is a member of the history of popular music.
It is a member of the UK top 100 album chart.
It is a member of the history of popular music.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Eurovision Song Contest.
It occurred at the 50th anniversary celebration of the Eurovision Song Contest.
It occurred at the Polar Music awards.
It occurred at the BRIT Awards.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the filming of a Swedish TV special in May 1969.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United Kingdom.
PartOf: It is part of the history of popular music.
It is part of the UK top 100 album chart.
It is part of the history of popular music.
PerformedBy: It was performed by Agnetha Fältskog.
It was performed by Björn Ulvaeus.
It was performed by Benny Andersson.
It was performed by Anni-Frid Lyngstad.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Benny Andersson.
It was produced by Stig Anderson.
PublishedAs: It was published as ABBA Gold.
It was published as Mamma Mia!.
It was published as a film of the same name in 2008.
It was published as Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again in 2018.
It was published as Voyage on 5 November 2021.
It was published as ABBA Voyage.
PublishedBy: It was published by Polar Music.
It was published by Universal Music Group.
PublishedIn: It was published in the United Kingdom.
It was published in the West End.

それはスペイン語でヒット曲のコレクションを録音するなど、ラテンアメリカで大きな成功を収めたという特徴があります。
